description: Recreate the morning sky over Chelyabinsk, Russia (55.15° N, 61.40° E, 210m) on February 15, 2013, at 03:20:00 UTC to demonstrate the 'solar blind spot' during the meteor impact. Configure the view with Atmosphere, Azimuthal Grid, and Cardinal Points enabled. Center the Sun in the viewport and save a screenshot to ~/Pictures/stellarium/. Write an incident analysis note to ~/Desktop/chelyabinsk_glare_analysis.txt explaining how the meteor's trajectory aligned with the morning solar glare, ensuring the text includes 'Chelyabinsk', 'Sun' or 'solar', and '2013'.

metadata.data_source: NASA JPL / CNEOS Chelyabinsk Event Data
